pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: CMRX surged 67.5% from prior close ($5.04) to trigger-day open ($8.40) on 2025-03-05. Narrative Agent classified the catalyst as VAGUE: a generic third-party market-research report (GlobeNewswire, DelveInsight) mentioning CMRX as one of 10+ companies in dopamine antagonist space, with no concrete clinical trial, partnership, regulatory milestone, or financing announcement. Alt-Data Verification Agent verdict: NARRATIVE_CONTRADICTED. Structural absence of hard-to-fake signals is the dominant bear thesis: (1) SEC filings unavailable (CIK not found) — cannot verify company identity or regulatory status. (2) Clinical trial registry data unavailable — cannot validate pipeline maturity. (3) Regulatory filing footprint unavailable — cannot confirm FDA status. (4) Insider Form 4 unavailable — no insider conviction signal. (5) Institutional 13F unavailable — no smart-money positioning visible. (6) Wikipedia pageviews are for unrelated technical standard (RTCM_SC-104), not company-specific attention. (7) Analyst sentiment (5 Buy, 1 Strong Buy as of 2025-03-01) is T2 descriptor-only and predates surge by 3+ days. (8) Fundamental deterioration is severe: Q3 2024 revenue $26k (vs $70k estimate = 63% miss), net margin -41,691%, EPS -$0.26. Valuation metrics distorted (PE -8.67, P/S 3778.7). Surge-Short Agent verdict: VALID short thesis. Risk Agent: NO HARD VETO TRIPPED (lookahead_safe=true, data_after_cutoff_used=false, no critical data-quality flags). Macro regime is Overheat/Moderately Restrictive with strict_quality_and_valuation_caution discipline, which argues against speculative biotech, but does not mechanically veto surge-short entry. PREPONDERANCE OF EVIDENCE: The surge is momentum-driven narrative arbitrage in a micro-cap biotech with deteriorating fundamentals and no verifiable catalyst footprint. The absence of required SEC filings for a listed company claiming major therapeutic development is itself a bear-thesis signal under §13.6 SOFT_REASONING. The vague narrative combined with structural absence of sector-expected alt-data (SEC filings, clinical registry, regulatory footprint) supports a short entry at fixed 0.5% position size per §4.8.
invalidation_conditions: If SEC 8-K filing is subsequently discovered confirming genuine clinical trial milestone, partnership, or FDA event dated on or before 2025-03-05, short thesis weakens materially.
If next quarter revenue exceeds $50M (vs $26k in Q3 2024), indicating real commercial traction, thesis is invalidated.
If insider Form 4 filings show significant insider buying at or above surge price, confidence in short thesis declines.
If clinical trial registry (ClinicalTrials.gov) entries for CMRX dopamine antagonist programs exist and are dated before 2025-03-05, absence pattern is broken.
risk_flags: Micro-cap liquidity risk: volume 2.03M shares on surge day; position sizing respects liquidity constraints at 0.5%.
Biotech binary event risk: clinical trial results, FDA decisions, or partnership announcements could reverse thesis rapidly.
Short-squeeze risk: low float, high short interest potential; borrow availability and cost not observable in packet per §5.16.
Macro headwind: regime=Overheat/Moderately Restrictive; equity_discipline=strict_quality_and_valuation_caution argues against speculative biotech.
Narrative momentum: dopamine antagonist market narrative is real; if company executes, thesis fails. Current evidence does not support execution yet.
SEC filing gap: inability to confirm catalyst via 8-K is red flag, but absence of CIK in adapter may reflect data-provider limitation, not company delisting.
Upstream sleeve position data not provided; cannot independently verify §4.8 short-position caps (5% single / 10% sleeve) before execution.
suggested_improvements: Obtain direct SEC EDGAR lookup for CMRX CIK to verify whether 8-K or other filings exist post-surge.
Cross-check company website / investor relations for press releases dated 2025-03-04 or 2025-03-05.
Verify clinical trial registry (clinicaltrials.gov) for any CMRX-sponsored trials and enrollment status.
Obtain insider Form 4 filings directly from SEC to assess insider confidence.
Monitor next earnings call (if scheduled) for management commentary on revenue trajectory and pipeline.
Provide upstream sleeve position data to enable §4.8 short-position cap verification before execution.
Verify borrow availability and cost for CMRX short position per §5.16.

narrative_event.reasoning_summary: CATALYST CLASSIFICATION: The single news item (published 2025-03-04, GlobeNewswire) is a market-research report titled 'Dopamine Receptor Antagonists Clinical Trial Pipeline Analysis' that mentions Chimerix (CMRX) as one of 10+ key companies in the dopamine receptor antagonist therapeutic space. The article text is generic market-sizing language ('increasing prevalence of psychiatric disorders', 'rising demand for more effective treatments') with no specific clinical trial data, regulatory milestones, partnership announcements, or financial commitments tied to CMRX. The 'insights_for_ticker' sentiment is marked 'positive' with reasoning that 'Chimerix is one of the key companies working on developing dopamine receptor antagonist drugs', but this is a VAGUE INFERENCE from a third-party market report, not a concrete company claim. SECTOR-AWARE REASONING: Chimerix is a biotech/pharma company. For such a company, legitimate catalysts would include: FDA IND/NDA filings, clinical trial enrollment updates, partnership announcements with named partners and financial terms, regulatory approvals, or earnings beats with revenue guidance. The present evidence shows: (1) Q3 2024 revenue of $26k (vs. $70k estimate) — a massive miss; (2) no SEC filings available (cik_not_found); (3) no GitHub activity (unmapped); (4) no insider Form 4 activity (cik_not_found); (5) analyst sentiment has IMPROVED from 0 Buy/5 Hold/1 Sell (May 2024) to 1 Strong Buy/4 Buy/4 Hold/0 Sell (Mar 2025), but this is DESCRIPTOR-ONLY per §11.13 and does not constitute a catalyst. PRICE ACTION CONTEXT: Stock returned +6.2% in 5d, +30.5% in 20d, +476.5% in 60d. The Wikipedia pageviews for 'RTCM_SC-104' (a technical standard, not CMRX) show modest activity (4–34 views/day, peak 34 on 2025-02-28). This is NOISE, not a catalyst signal. NARRATIVE SPECIFICITY: The news item is VAGUE. It does not name a specific clinical trial, provide enrollment numbers, announce a partnership, disclose a financing, or cite a regulatory filing. It is a generic market-research report that happens to mention CMRX as one of many companies. The company claim is IMPLICIT and UNSUPPORTED: 'Chimerix is developing dopamine receptor antagonist drugs' — but no concrete evidence (trial registry entry, press release, SEC filing, partnership announcement) is present in the packet to corroborate this. ABSENCE PATTERN: For a biotech company claiming active drug development, the structural absence of SEC filings (cik_not_found), insider activity (Form 4 unavailable), and clinical trial registry entries (not in packet) is BEAR-THESIS-LEANING per §13.6 SOFT_REASONING. The company's Q3 2024 revenue of $26k suggests minimal commercial traction. INFORMATION INTEGRITY: The news source is T2 (GlobeNewswire, a reputable wire service), but the underlying content is a third-party market report, not an official company disclosure. information_integrity_assessment.primary_source_found = false; corroboration_status = uncorroborated. NDI cannot be computed (only 1 source within 24h window). VERDICT: The catalyst is VAGUE. It is a generic market-research mention with no concrete specificity, no official company claim, and no corroborating evidence. The analyst sentiment improvement is a DESCRIPTOR, not a catalyst. The price move (+476% in 60d) is not explained by this news item (published 2025-03-04, after the bulk of the move). This is a MOMENTUM / NARRATIVE-FOLLOWING scenario, not a VALUE-CREATION catalyst.
invalidation_conditions: If a subsequent SEC 8-K or press release from Chimerix announces a specific clinical trial, partnership, or regulatory milestone dated on or before 2025-03-05, the vague verdict would be invalidated and the catalyst would be reclassified as 'supported' or 'credible_catalyst'.

If clinical trial registry (ClinicalTrials.gov) entries for CMRX dopamine receptor antagonist programs exist and are dated before 2025-03-05, the absence pattern would be broken and evidence_sufficiency would improve.
If the company's actual sector classification is NOT biotech/pharma (e.g., if CMRX is a software or services company), the sector-aware reasoning would need to be recalibrated.

alt_data_verify.decision_or_assessment: Narrative-Price Dislocation Verification: CMRX surge narrative vs. alternative-data corroboration
alt_data_verify.reasoning_summary: CMRX exhibits a 476.5% 60-day return and 30.5% 20-day return as of 2025-03-04, triggering surge-short candidate classification. The public narrative centers on dopamine receptor antagonist market opportunity (GlobeNewswire, 2025-03-04, T2 source). Alternative-data verification reveals: (1) Wikipedia pageviews for 'RTCM_SC-104' (unrelated technical standard, not CMRX company article) show modest activity (4–34 views/day, peak 2025-02-28 at 34 views), declining to 25 views by decision date—this is NOT company-specific attention but rather incidental article traffic. (2) FMP analyst sentiment shows 5 Buy + 1 Strong Buy as of 2025-03-01 (T2 descriptor-only, not a gate). (3) Fundamental snapshot (Q3 2024, filed 2024-11-07): revenue $26k, net income -$22.9M, operating income -$24.8M, EPS -$0.26, gross margin 100% (anomalous), net margin -87,996%. (4) Valuation: PE -8.67, PB 6.30, EV/EBITDA -7.99, price-to-sales 3,778.7—all distorted by negative earnings. (5) Critical absence: SEC filings unavailable (CIK not found), insider form4 unavailable, 13F unavailable, GitHub unmapped. For a biotech/pharma company claiming dopamine antagonist pipeline, the structural absence of SEC filing confirmation, clinical trial registry data, and regulatory footprint is INFORMATIVE ABSENCE. The Wikipedia pageviews are NOT company-specific attention (article is about RTCM technical standard, not Chimerix). The surge narrative lacks hard-to-fake corroboration: no SEC 8-K, no insider activity visible, no clinical trial registry rows, no regulatory filing index. The analyst sentiment (5 Buy) is T2 descriptor-only and predates the surge (as_of 2025-03-01, before 2025-03-04 price spike). Conclusion: Structural absence of sector-expected alt-data (SEC filings, clinical registry, regulatory footprint) relative to public narrative of pipeline opportunity is bear-thesis evidence. The Wikipedia pageviews are noise, not signal. Analyst sentiment is stale relative to price move.

alt_data_verify.narrative_price_gap_rationale: Public narrative claims CMRX is a key company in dopamine receptor antagonist market with pipeline opportunity (GlobeNewswire, 2025-03-04). Price surge: 476.5% over 60 days, 30.5% over 20 days. Alternative-data corroboration is structurally absent: (1) SEC filings unavailable (CIK not found) — cannot verify company identity or regulatory status. (2) Clinical trial registry data unavailable (biotech_clinical_adapter not_connected) — cannot validate pipeline maturity. (3) Regulatory filing footprint unavailable (default_sec_gdelt_adapter not_connected) — cannot confirm FDA status. (4) Insider activity unavailable (Form 4, CIK not found) — no insider conviction signal. (5) Institutional ownership unavailable (13F, not_available_on_current_plan) — cannot assess smart-money positioning. (6) Wikipedia pageviews are for unrelated technical standard (RTCM_SC-104), not company-specific attention. (7) Analyst sentiment (5 Buy) is T2 descriptor-only and predates surge by 3+ days. (8) Fundamental snapshot (Q3 2024): -$22.9M net income on $26k revenue, -$0.26 EPS, 100% gross margin (anomalous). Structural absence of sector-expected alt-data (SEC filings, clinical registry, regulatory footprint) relative to public narrative is bear-thesis evidence. The surge lacks hard-to-fake corroboration.

surge_short.reasoning_summary: CMRX surged ~67% from prior close ($5.04) to trigger-day open ($8.40) on 2025-03-05. The public narrative centers on dopamine receptor antagonist therapeutics market opportunity. However, structural-absence evidence is the dominant bear signal: (1) No SEC 8-K filing available to confirm the catalyst event, despite mandatory disclosure requirement for listed companies. (2) No insider Form 4 activity. (3) Fundamental deterioration is severe: Q3 2024 revenue only $26k (vs $70k estimate), net margin -41,691%, EPS -$0.26. (4) Valuation metrics are distorted (PE=-8.67, P/S=3778.7) due to near-zero revenue. (5) Wikipedia pageview attention on RTCM_SC-104 (unrelated technical standard) shows modest baseline activity (~18 views/day) with no spike correlated to the surge date. (6) Analyst sentiment is stable (1 SB, 4 B, 4 H as of Mar 1) and unchanged from Jan 2025, suggesting no recent positive catalyst recognized by sell-side. (7) The single news item is a generic market-analysis press release (GlobeNewswire, Delveinsight), not a company-specific announcement. (8) No corroborating alt-data (GitHub, SEC filings, insider activity) supports a real value-creation event. The surge appears to be momentum-driven narrative arbitrage in a micro-cap biotech with deteriorating fundamentals and no verifiable catalyst footprint. The absence of required SEC filings for a listed company claiming a major therapeutic development is itself a bear-thesis signal under §13.6 SOFT_REASONING and the structural-absence-as-signal pattern.
invalidation_conditions: If an SEC 8-K filing is subsequently discovered confirming a genuine clinical trial milestone, partnership, or FDA event dated on or before 2025-03-05, the short thesis weakens materially.
If revenue guidance or actual revenue in next quarter exceeds $50M (vs $26k in Q3 2024), indicating real commercial traction, the thesis is invalidated.
If insider Form 4 filings show significant insider buying at or above the surge price, confidence in the short thesis would decline.
If institutional ownership (13F) shows major hedge fund accumulation post-surge, momentum may persist longer than fundamentals justify.

pm.reasoning_summary: CMRX surged 67.5% from prior close ($5.04) to trigger-day open ($8.40) on 2025-03-05. Narrative Agent classified the catalyst as VAGUE: a generic third-party market-research report (GlobeNewswire, DelveInsight) mentioning CMRX as one of 10+ companies in dopamine antagonist space, with no concrete clinical trial, partnership, regulatory milestone, or financing announcement. Alt-Data Verification Agent verdict: NARRATIVE_CONTRADICTED. Structural absence of hard-to-fake signals is the dominant bear thesis: (1) SEC filings unavailable (CIK not found) — cannot verify company identity or regulatory status. (2) Clinical trial registry data unavailable — cannot validate pipeline maturity. (3) Regulatory filing footprint unavailable — cannot confirm FDA status. (4) Insider Form 4 unavailable — no insider conviction signal. (5) Institutional 13F unavailable — no smart-money positioning visible. (6) Wikipedia pageviews are for unrelated technical standard (RTCM_SC-104), not company-specific attention. (7) Analyst sentiment (5 Buy, 1 Strong Buy as of 2025-03-01) is T2 descriptor-only and predates surge by 3+ days. (8) Fundamental deterioration is severe: Q3 2024 revenue $26k (vs $70k estimate = 63% miss), net margin -41,691%, EPS -$0.26. Valuation metrics distorted (PE -8.67, P/S 3778.7). Surge-Short Agent verdict: VALID short thesis. Risk Agent: NO HARD VETO TRIPPED (lookahead_safe=true, data_after_cutoff_used=false, no critical data-quality flags). Macro regime is Overheat/Moderately Restrictive with strict_quality_and_valuation_caution discipline, which argues against speculative biotech, but does not mechanically veto surge-short entry. PREPONDERANCE OF EVIDENCE: The surge is momentum-driven narrative arbitrage in a micro-cap biotech with deteriorating fundamentals and no verifiable catalyst footprint. The absence of required SEC filings for a listed company claiming major therapeutic development is itself a bear-thesis signal under §13.6 SOFT_REASONING. The vague narrative combined with structural absence of sector-expected alt-data (SEC filings, clinical registry, regulatory footprint) supports a short entry at fixed 0.5% position size per §4.8.
invalidation_conditions: If SEC 8-K filing is subsequently discovered confirming genuine clinical trial milestone, partnership, or FDA event dated on or before 2025-03-05, short thesis weakens materially.
If next quarter revenue exceeds $50M (vs $26k in Q3 2024), indicating real commercial traction, thesis is invalidated.
If insider Form 4 filings show significant insider buying at or above surge price, confidence in short thesis declines.
If clinical trial registry (ClinicalTrials.gov) entries for CMRX dopamine antagonist programs exist and are dated before 2025-03-05, absence pattern is broken.
